title: Axonal Proteome Analysis and Western Blot via APEX2 Labeling
author: Chuyu Chen

steps.title: Mice were anesthetized with ketamine and...
steps.instruction: Mice were anesthetized with ketamine and underwent transcardial perfusion with 10 ml of ice-cold cutting solution saturated with 95% O 2 and 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: Cutting solution recipe
steps.instruction: Cutting solution recipe: 110 mM choline, 2.5 mM KCl, 1.25 mM monosodium phosphate, 10 mM glucose, 7 mM MgCl 2 , 0.5 mM CaCl 2 , 1.3 mM NaH 2 PO 4 , and 25 mM sodium bicarbonate.

steps.title: Prepare coronal slices (300 µm) with Leica VT1200S
steps.instruction: Prepare coronal slices (300 µm) with Leica VT1200S.

steps.title: Transfer slices to a glass dish...
steps.instruction: Transfer slices to a glass dish containing artificial cerebrospinal fluid (aCSF), supplemented with 0.5 mM biotin-phenol. The aCSF was continuously saturated with 95% O 2 and 5% CO 2 . incubate for 60mins, RT

steps.title: Add 1 mM H 2 O...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mM H 2 O 2 to the aCSF, 5 minutes, RT.

steps.title: The slices were rapidly transferred to...
steps.instruction: The slices were rapidly transferred to a separate glass dish containing quenching aCSF (aCSF supplemented with 10 mM Trolox, 20 mM sodium ascorbate, and 10 mM sodium azide) 5 minutes, RT

steps.title: Homogenize frozen APEX2-labeled tissues in 0
steps.instruction: Homogenize frozen APEX2-labeled tissues in 0.75ml ice-cold tissue lysis buffer on ice using a glass Dounce homogenizer with 30 strokes of both A and B pestles.

steps.title: Rapidly dissect the slices in ice-cold quenching aCSF
steps.instruction: Rapidly dissect the slices in ice-cold quenching aCSF. Flash-frozen the collected tissue on dry ice and stored at -80°C until further western blot or proteomic experiments.

steps.title: Tissue lysis buffer recipe
steps.instruction: Tissue lysis buffer recipe: 50 mM Tris (pH 8.0), 150 mM NaCl, 10 mM EDTA, 1% Triton X-100, 5 mM Trolox, 10 mM sodium ascorbate, 10 mM sodium azide, and Halt protease and phosphatase inhibitor cocktail (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: Add 39 μl of 10% SDS...
steps.instruction: Add 39 μl of 10% SDS to achieve a final concentration of 0.5%, incubate on a rotary wheel for 15 minutes, 4°C

steps.title: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Transfer supernatans to a new prechilled...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatans to a new prechilled Eppendorf tube for trichloroacetic acid (TCA) precipitation (for LC-MS) or stored at −80°C (for western blot).

steps.title: Add equal volume of ice-cold 55%...
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of ice-cold 55% trichloroacetic acid (TCA) and incubate on ice for 30 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend protein pellets in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend protein pellets in 1 ml of acetone (prechilled to -20°C)

steps.title: Remove any residual acetone, the protein...
steps.instruction: Remove any residual acetone, the protein pellets were resuspended (500ml) in the Urea Dissolve Buffer

steps.title: sonication for 1 minute, gentle agitation...
steps.instruction: sonication for 1 minute, gentle agitation on an orbital shaker for 1 hour at RT

steps.title: A small aliquot (5%) of the...
steps.instruction: A small aliquot (5%) of the resuspended protein was flash-frozen and stored at -80°C. Diluted the rest of the sample (95%) with water to achieve a final concentration of 4 M urea and 0.5% SDS.

steps.title: Resuspend streptavidin magnetic beads (Thermo Fisher #88817)
steps.instruction: Resuspend streptavidin magnetic beads (Thermo Fisher #88817)

steps.title: 1) AAV5-CAG-DIO-APEX2-NES (Addgene plasmid #79907, virus...
steps.instruction: 1) AAV5-CAG-DIO-APEX2-NES (Addgene plasmid #79907, virus packaged by VectorBiolabs; titer: 3.8x10¹² GC/ml), delivered bilaterally for APEX2 proximity labeling. 2)The homozygous Cre-dependent APEX2 mouse line was crossed with the DatCre line to achieve expression of APEX2 in dopamine neurons of the substantia nigra compacta (SNc).

steps.title: Resuspend streptavidin magnetic beads in ice-cold Urea Detergent Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend streptavidin magnetic beads in ice-cold Urea Detergent Wash Buffer

steps.title: Incubate overnight on a rotary wheel...
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight on a rotary wheel at 4°C for 14-18 hours.

steps.title: Discard unbound supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard unbound supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 1 ml of Urea Wash Buffer and transfer to a new tube.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 200 μl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 200 μl of Urea Wash Buffer and transfer to a new tube.

steps.title: Determining protein concentration of frozen tissue...
steps.instruction: Determining protein concentration of frozen tissue lysates using the BCA assay (Thermo Scientific) 10 μg APEX2 labeled tissue lysate (from step 12) was loaded into 4–12% NuPAGE Bis-Tris PAGE (Invitrogen) as input.

steps.title: Resuspend the streptavidin beads in ~20...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the streptavidin beads in ~20 μl of 1× SDS sample buffer supplemented with 20 mM DTT and 2 mM biotin.

steps.title: Immediately placed the beads onto a...
steps.instruction: Immediately placed the beads onto a magnetic rack and the entire sample was immediately loaded into 4–12% NuPAGE Bis-Tris PAGE (Invitrogen)

steps.title: Incubate the membranes with primary antibodies...
steps.instruction: Incubate the membranes with primary antibodies specific for streptavidin-HRP (1:1000, Abcam), LRRK2/Dardarin clone N137/6 (1:1000, NeuroMab), Th (1:1000, Millipore), Rab3a (1:1000, Sigma), Rab3c (1:1000, Proteintech) overnight at 4°C

steps.title: Incubate the membranes with secondary anti-mouse...
steps.instruction: Incubate the membranes with secondary anti-mouse or anti-rabbit antibodies (1:2000, Thermo Scientific) for 1 hour at room temperature.

steps.title: Incubate the membranes with Immobilon ECL...
steps.instruction: Incubate the membranes with Immobilon ECL Ultra Western HRP Substrate (Millipore) for 3 minutes

steps.title: Chemiluminescent blots were imaged using the...
steps.instruction: Chemiluminescent blots were imaged using the iBright imaging system (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: Samples were solubilized and digested overnight...
steps.instruction: Samples were solubilized and digested overnight with sequencing grade trypsin (Promega) in a urea-containing denaturation buffer. Each sample is the eluted proteins from one mouse

steps.title: Beads were collected on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Beads were collected on a magnetic rack, supernatant was transferred to a new tube and used for the clean-up.

steps.title: Purification for mass spectrometry was carried...
steps.instruction: Purification for mass spectrometry was carried out using Oasis HLB μElution Plate 30μm plate (WATERS) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Peptides were dried down to complete...
steps.instruction: Peptides were dried down to complete dryness using a SpeedVac system and dissolved in LC solvent A (1 % acetonitrile in water with 0.1 % formic acid (FA)) containing Biognosys’ iRT-peptide mix for retention time calibration.

steps.title: Peptide concentrations in mass spectrometry ready...
steps.instruction: Peptide concentrations in mass spectrometry ready samples were measured using the mBCA assay (Thermo Scientific Pierce).

steps.title: For DIA LC-MS/MS measurements, peptides were...
steps.instruction: For DIA LC-MS/MS measurements, peptides were injected on an in-house packed reversed phase column on a Thermo Scientific EASY-nLC 1200 nano-liquid chromatography system connected to a Thermo Scientific Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer equipped with a Nanospray Flex ion source and a FAIMS Pro ion mobility device (Thermo Scientific).

steps.title: LC solvents were A
steps.instruction: LC solvents were A: water with 0.1 % FA; B: 80 % acetonitrile, 0.1 % FA in water. The nonlinear LC gradient was 1 – 50 % solvent B in 171 minutes followed by a column washing step in 90 % B for 7 minutes, anda final equilibration step of 1 % B for 0.5 column volumes at 64 °C with a flow rate set to a ramp between 450 to 271 nl/min (min 0: 450 nl/min, min 172: 271 nl/min, washing at 400 nl/min).

steps.title: The DIA mass spectrometric data were...
steps.instruction: The DIA mass spectrometric data were analyzed using Spectronaut software (Biognosys,version 19.0). The false discovery rate on peptide and protein level was set to 1 %.

steps.title: A mouse UniProt
steps.instruction: A mouse UniProt .fasta database (Mus musculus, 2024-07-01) was used for the search engine, allowing for 2 missed cleavages, carbamidomethylation of cysteine as fixed modification and up to 5 variablemodifications (N-terminal acetylation, methionine oxidation, deamidation of asparagine or glutamine and phosphorylation at serine or threonine or tyrosine).

steps.title: HRM mass spectrometric data were analyzed...
steps.instruction: HRM mass spectrometric data were analyzed using Spectronaut software (Biognosys, version 19.0). The false discovery rate on peptide and protein level was set to 1 %, data was filtered using row-basedextraction.

steps.title: The directDIA+ library generated in this...
steps.instruction: The directDIA+ library generated in this project was used for the analysis. The HRM measurements analyzed with Spectronaut were normalized using global median normalization.

steps.title: Proteins that were not enriched (enriched
steps.instruction: Proteins that were not enriched (enriched: q-value < 0.05, log2fc > 0) in Anxa1 Cre ; Lrrk2 G2019S vehiclevs. non-transgenic were removed from the dataset of samples from SNc and striatum, respectively. The filtered dataset was used for further analysis.

steps.title: For testing of differential protein abundance,...
steps.instruction: For testing of differential protein abundance, protein intensities for each protein were analyzed using a two-sample Student’s t-test. The following thresholds were applied for candidate identification: p-value < 0.05; absolute average log2 ratio > 0.58 (fold-change > 1.5).

steps.title: Distance in heat maps was calculated...
steps.instruction: Distance in heat maps was calculated using the “manhattan” method, the clustering using “ward.D” for both axes. Principal component analysis was conducted in R using prcomp and a modified ggbiplotfunction for plotting. Functional analysis was performed using String-db (string-db.org, version 12.0)

steps.title: Phospho-site summarization was carried out using...
steps.instruction: Phospho-site summarization was carried out using a PTM localization probability cutoff of > 0.75 and linear model as aggregation type.

steps.title: For testing of differential phospho-site abundance,...
steps.instruction: For testing of differential phospho-site abundance, phosphosite intensities for each protein were analyzed using a two sample Student’s t test. The following thresholds were applied for candidate identification: p- value <0.05; absolute average log2 ratio > 0.58 (fold change > 1.5).

steps.title: quickly extract the brain and place...
steps.instruction: quickly extract the brain and place in ice-cold cutting solution saturated with 95% O 2 and 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: artificial cerebrospinal fluid (aCSF) recipe
steps.instruction: artificial cerebrospinal fluid (aCSF) recipe: 2.5 mM KCl, 10 mM glucose, 125.2 mM NaCl, 0.3 mM NaH 2 PO 4 , 1.3 mM MgCl 2 , 2.4 mM CaCl 2 , 26 mM NaHCO 3 , and 0.3 mM KH 2 PO 4 , supplemented with 0.5 mM biotin-phenol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 21,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Repeat steps 15-16, for a total of 4 washes
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 15-16, for a total of 4 washes

steps.title: Urea Dissolve Buffer recipe
steps.instruction: Urea Dissolve Buffer recipe: 8 M urea, 1% sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), 100 mM sodium phosphate (pH 8), and 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate (NH 4 HCO 3 ).

steps.title: wash in Urea Detergent Wash Buffer, 15 mins, 4°C x3
steps.instruction: wash in Urea Detergent Wash Buffer, 15 mins, 4°C x3

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mg/50ml streptavidin magnetic beads (from step 20.2) to each sample from step 18.3

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnetic stand for 1 min
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnetic stand for 1 min

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 1 ml of Urea Detergent Wash Buffer and transfer to a new tube.

steps.title: Urea Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Urea Wash Buffer: 4 M urea, 100 mM sodium phosphate, pH 8

steps.title: wash in Urea Wash Buffer, 10 mins, RT x3
steps.instruction: wash in Urea Wash Buffer, 10 mins, RT x3

steps.title: Remove the buffer using a magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the buffer using a magnetic stand. flash-frozen the beads and stored at -80°C for western blot or LC-MS.

steps.title: Boil samples for 5 min at...
steps.instruction: Boil samples for 5 min at 95°C to elute biotinylated proteins.

steps.title: Transfer the NuPAGE to membranes using...
steps.instruction: Transfer the NuPAGE to membranes using the iBlot nitrocellulose membrane blotting system (Invitrogen)

steps.title: Wash membranes with TBS/Tween-20, 10 mins x3
steps.instruction: Wash membranes with TBS/Tween-20, 10 mins x3

steps.title: Wash membranes with TBS/Tween-20, 10 mins x3
steps.instruction: Wash membranes with TBS/Tween-20, 10 mins x3

steps.title: wash in Urea Detergent Wash Buffer, 10 mins, RT x3
steps.instruction: wash in Urea Detergent Wash Buffer, 10 mins, RT x3

steps.title: Urea Detergent Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Urea Detergent Wash Buffer : 4 M urea, 0.5% SDS, 100 mM sodium phosphate, pH 8
